Not sure what you can do with Notion Agents? Here are 100 ideas
ACCESS THE FULL PROMPT LIBRARY HERE
OPENAI SIGNS $10 BILLION DEAL FOR COMPUTE
articles.summary: OpenAI signed a multi-year deal with Cerebras worth over $10 billion to receive 750 megawatts of compute through 2028. The partnership aims to boost inference speed for OpenAI's products.

articles.title: GEMINI INTRODUCES PERSONAL INTELLIGENCE
articles.summary: Gemini's Personal Intelligence beta, launching in the US, connects Google apps to personalize user experiences by securely linking Gmail, Photos, YouTube, and Search for tailored assistance. Users control app connections, enhancing the AI's ability to provide specific, informed responses. Privacy is prioritized, ensuring data remains secure and under user control while continuously refining functionality.

articles.title: AIRBNB POACHES FORMER META GENAI LEADER TO BE NEW TECHNOLOGY CHIEF
articles.summary: Airbnb appointed Ahmad Al-Dahle, former head of GenAI at Meta, as its new technology chief to enhance AI in travel and e-commerce. The company is shifting beyond short-term rentals, integrating new services, AI chatbots, and aiming to become a travel concierge. Al-Dahle's extensive experience with AI at Meta and Apple aligns with Airbnb's strategic focus on tech-driven growth.

articles.title: DOUBLING INFERENCE SPEED AT CHARACTER.AI
articles.summary: GPU workload tuning and hardware-level optimizations reduced latency and cost across Character.ai's systems and doubled production inference speed.

articles.title: UNDERSTANDING MANUS SANDBOX - YOUR CLOUD COMPUTER
SOFTWARE TOO CHEAP TO METER
articles.summary: AI coding agents are making software development nearly free, allowing for personalized applications tailored to individual needs. These agents, like Anthropic's Claude Code, can automate small projects that save time. While the technology is still developing, it promises rapid changes in software customization and productivity by 2026.

articles.title: HOW SHOPIFY AND GOOGLE BUILT THE UNIVERSAL COMMERCE PROTOCOL (UCP) SO AI AGENTS CAN TRANSACT WITH ANY MERCHANT
articles.summary: Shopify and Google co-developed an open standard for AI agents: the Universal Commerce Protocol (UCP) [12]. UCP defines the discovery and negotiation mechanisms between agent and merchant, and makes it simple for humans and agents to work together. Read more on the Shopify

articles.title: Engineering Blog
GPT-5.2-CODEX IS NOW AVAILABLE IN THE RESPONSES API
articles.summary: GPT-5.2-Codex is an upgraded version of GPT-5.2 optimized for agentic coding tasks in Codex and similar environments. It supports four levels of reasoning effort settings and has a 400,000-token context window. The model costs $1.75 per million input tokens and $14.00 per million output tokens. It is now available in the Responses API.

articles.title: TOOL SEARCH NOW IN CLAUDE CODE
articles.summary: MCP Tool Search is now rolling out in Claude Code. The feature allows Claude Code to dynamically load tools into context. Claude Code detects when MCP tool descriptions would use more than 10% of the context, and when triggered, tools are loaded via search instead of preloaded. MCP tools will otherwise work exactly as before.

articles.title: MINISTRAL 3 TECHNICAL REPORT
articles.summary: Ministral 3 is a new family of dense language models (3B, 8B, and 14B) optimized for low-resource environments, offering base, instruction-tuned, and reasoning variants. The models support image understanding and were trained using Cascade Distillation, an iterative distillation and pruning method.

articles.title: OPENAI'S HIDDEN CHATGPT TRANSLATE TOOL TAKES ON GOOGLE TRANSLATE
articles.summary: OpenAI is quietly rolling out a feature called ChatGPT Translate that looks very similar to Google Translate. It is unclear which model OpenAI is using for the feature. ChatGPT Translate is free to use and doesn't require a paid account. Users can keep the conversation going after the first translation. A screenshot of the feature is available in the article.

articles.title: ANTHROPIC IS MAKING A HUGE MISTAKE
articles.summary: Anthropic blocked opencode from the Claude Code API, causing frustration among users and encouraging them to switch models. This decision risks driving users to other providers by showcasing hostility against users who don't comply with strict usage policies. Blocking opencode is seen as a first step in enforcing rigid control, potentially alienating its user base.

articles.title: AI'S WAY COOLER TRILLION-DOLLAR OPPORTUNITY: VIBE GRAPHS
articles.summary: The emerging trillion-dollar opportunity lies in developing "vibes graphs" capturing ambient organizational sentiment. Current enterprise software tracks structured data but misses the nuanced vibes that influence decisions, leading to potential monetary losses. Startups building vibes-native infrastructure are poised to create competitive advantages over incumbents by capturing real-time emotional cues, unlocking deep insights into organizational dynamics.

articles.title: IS AI BECOMING ANOTHER DATA PLUMBING PROJECT?
articles.summary: 71% of data and AI teams spend 25%+ of their time on data connectivity. Learn how leaders are spending less time plumbing and more time developing in CData's 2026 State of AI Data Connectivity Report [19].

articles.title: GOOGLE TRENDS GETS GEMINI
articles.summary: Google revamped its Trends Explore page with Gemini AI, adding features that surface related search terms, auto-generate comparisons, and suggest follow-up queries.

articles.title: BUILDING AGENTS WITH THE GEMINI INTERACTIONS API
GAS TOWN DECODED
articles.summary: Steve Yegge's tool, "Gas Town," introduces a complex AI agent orchestration system with unique terminology.

articles.title: MICROSOFT ON TRACK TO SPEND $500 MILLION PER YEAR ON ANTHROPIC AI
articles.summary: Microsoft has been using Anthropic's AI to power more and more of its own products.
